name: Alto del Pino Loop
name_original: El Alto del Pino – circular desde Naval (ZZ-086)
description: This moderate 14.3-mile mountain biking route from Naval offers panoramic Pyrenees views, historical sites, and unique landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Naval traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and varied terrain, offering a mix of ascents and descents. The region features areas of pine forests and rocky outcrops, providing diverse riding conditions. Mountain bikers can expect routes that navigate through natural landscapes, with some trails offering views of the surrounding countryside. The area's topography supports both moderate and more challenging mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Naval The most popular mountain bike trail is Alto del Pino Loop, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through a forested area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Abizanda – Ermita de San Benito loop from Abizanda, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This trail offers challenging climbs and descents through varied natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Abizanda loop from Abizanda, a 14.4 miles (23.2 km) trail leading through a mix of open and wooded areas, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Naval is defined by its rolling hills, pine forests, and rocky sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult challenges. The routes in Naval are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Naval's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Naval?
A: Naval offers a good selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 40 trails available. These routes traverse rolling hills, pine forests, and rocky sections, providing diverse riding conditions.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Naval?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in Naval are rated as moderate, with 28 routes falling into this category. There are also 14 difficult trails for experienced riders seeking more challenging climbs and descents. Currently, there are no easy trails listed.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Naval?
A: While there are no trails specifically rated as 'easy' in Naval, many of the moderate routes can be suitable for beginners with some prior experience. These trails offer a mix of ascents and descents through natural landscapes. It's always recommended to check the route details, including elevation gain and distance, before heading out.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Naval?
A: For advanced riders looking for a challenge, Naval offers several difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5329765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abizanda – Ermita de San Benito loop from Abizanda&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.5 miles (36.2 km) long and features significant elevation changes. Another difficult trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17546256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Naval – Abizanda loop from Naval&lt;/a&gt;, covering 28.1 miles (28.1 km) with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Naval?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Naval are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975555420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Pino Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Naval – Peña Robles loop from Naval&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Naval?
A: The mountain bike trails in Naval are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse riding conditions and scenic views.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Naval?
A: Mountain bike trails around Naval offer diverse scenery, characterized by rolling hills, areas of pine forests, and rocky outcrops. Riders can expect routes that navigate through natural landscapes, with some trails providing views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: While riding, you might encounter various points of interest in the wider Naval area. Notable attractions include the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2980198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alquézar&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/975037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Villacantal&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1208930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Gregorio&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samitier Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Naval?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Naval generally depends on the specific weather conditions, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, while winters might bring cooler temperatures, though many trails remain accessible year-round.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Naval?
A: While some routes are more popular, Naval has over 40 trails, offering opportunities to find less crowded options, especially outside of peak times. Exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes might also lead to a quieter experience. Checking komoot for recent tour activity can help identify less frequented paths.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Naval?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Naval varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975555420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Pino Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 26 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5329765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abizanda – Ermita de San Benito loop from Abizanda&lt;/a&gt; can take over 3 hours 50 minutes.
tips: This town, once an essential crossing point in the connection between Somontano and Sobrabre and France, has been known for its salt mines since Muslim times. It therefore has some interesting salt warehouses or "alfolíes".
The 40-meter-high rectangular donchón tower is one of the most unique elements of this town, which draws attention even from afar. The entire population grew as a result of the castle.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The El Alto del Pino – circular desde Naval (ZZ-086) route is designed for mountain biking and generally takes around 2 hours and 26 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for mountain biking on this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for mountain biking, though the physical exertion is considered 'Hard'. The terrain is diverse, with approximately 43% consisting of singletrack, primarily on conglomerate surfaces. You'll encounter 'stones and history' through ancient salt flats and unique old stone constructions, with some sections involving winding roads.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning panoramic views of the Pyrenees from the summit of Alto del Pino (1,018 meters). Other scenic highlights include Peña Montañesa and the El Grado and Mediano reservoirs. You'll also pass by the 11th-century Castle of Abizanda and the historic Salinas de Rolda in Naval, where you can even swim.

faqs.question: Where can I start the route and find parking?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers flexibility with starting points available in both Naval and Abizanda. Both villages provide amenities like bars and restaurants, making them convenient places to begin your ride. Parking is generally available in these settlements.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is rated as moderate for mountain biking, its 'Hard' physical exertion rating suggests it might be challenging for absolute beginners. Riders should have some experience with mountain biking, especially given the 43% singletrack and conglomerate surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. Notably, even in late winter, the conditions can be quite warm. For the most pleasant experience, consider spring or autumn to avoid the peak summer heat, though the lush and green landscape is appealing in many seasons.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the El Alto del Pino – circular desde Naval (ZZ-086) trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is part of the Zona Zero network. While specific dog policies for this exact trail aren't detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash on mountain biking trails to ensure their safety and that of other trail users, as well as to protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail in Zona Zero Pirineos?
faqs.answer: The El Alto del Pino – circular desde Naval (ZZ-086) trail is part of the 'Zona Zero' network in Sobrarbe, Huesca, which recovers and prepares traditional paths for cycling. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for individual rides on these trails. However, it's always good practice to check the official Zona Zero website for any updates or specific regulations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sendero Histórico Variante Sur, Senderos del Somontano, Cruz De La Trinidad, Llegada Abizanda, Entre Pinos Por El Alto Del Pino, and the GR 45, among others.

faqs.question: Are e-bikes permitted on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, e-bikes are permitted on the El Alto del Pino – circular desde Naval (ZZ-086) trail, allowing more riders to enjoy the historical and scenic landscapes of the Alto Aragón region.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Naval and Abizanda, which serve as potential starting points for the circular route, offer amenities such as bars and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride.
